text: We present the case of a sixty-year-old male under dual anti-platelet therapy who presents to General Surgery Clinic for surgical opinion regarding a gastric mass near the gastroesophageal junction that was found incidentally unimagined for cardiac surgery for mitral valve regurgitation. The patient was asymptomatic with the exception of occasional dysphagia. A CT scan revealed a possible gastroesophageal junction partial exophytic gastrointestinal stromal tumor, and the abdominal MRI confirmed a well-circumscribed mass arising from the lesser curvature of the stomach in the G-junction. The endoscopic ultrasound showed a three point eight centimetres bilobular and subepithelial lesion arising from within the muscular propia, which appearance was of a stromal cell neoplasm. Decision was made to undergo a robotic assisted transgastric enucleation of the smocosal lesion at the GE junction.

The surgeon begins the procedure by mobilizing the gastroesophageal junction. The left crux is dissected off the angle of His, and the pars flaccida is opened to expose the right crux. No hiatal hernia is observed. The left gastric artery is identified, and the lesser sac is centered inferiorly to it. A four-centimeter mass is visible in the lesser curvature, approximately two centimeters from the gastroesophageal junction, bulging towards the upper edge of the pancreas.

An intraoperative endoscopy confirms the presence of the lesion seen on MRI and endoscopic ultrasound. The surgeon carefully enucleates the lesion from the surrounding gastric tissues using robotic assistance.

Aleomioma is a benign smooth muscle tumor that can occur in any organ, being the most common submucosal tumor of the stomach. Over the years, the incidence of these lesions is rising due to a higher level of suspicions exhibited by clinicians and the availability of diagnostic tools.
The surgeon encounters an area where the tumor is firmly adherent to the stomach mucosa. To facilitate removal of the tumor with its capsule, the mucosa is carefully opened.

After completing the enucleation, the surgeon sutures the mucosa with 3-0 PDS sutures and re-approximates the muscular layer using 2-0 VLOG sutures. Hemostasis is confirmed, and an intraoperative endoscopy shows no leaks, with the gastroesophageal junction visibly wide open.

Operative room time was one hundred and fifty minutes. The upper gastrointestinal contrast study showed no extra-luminal contrast and the final pathological report described a leiomyoma with positive neoplastic cells for desmin and actin and negative immunosuchemistry for S-one hundred, CD-thirty-four and CD-one hundred seventeen. The patient was readmitted on post-operative day six due to a gastrointestinal hemorrhage. Hemostatic spray was applied by EGT and anticoagulation was discontinued for one week.
